description: Conceptual map illustrating the characteristics of "INTERTIDAL FLATS". The map shows a main body of water (dark blue) with a white arrow indicating "SEA INFLUENCE" directed towards an extensive grey area labelled "INTERTIDAL FLATS". This grey intertidal flat area is permeated by numerous smaller, branching water channels (light blue), connecting to the main dark blue water body. Green areas represent general land cover, and a yellow strip runs along the edge of the main water body, potentially representing a beach or dune. This schematic highlights the influence of the sea and the presence of tidal channels characteristic of intertidal flats, differentiating them from features like estuaries as discussed in the surrounding document context.
